title: Does the brain generate consciousness? Or is it a transceiver of consciousness?
summary: This interview segment discusses the relationship between the brain and consciousness, exploring whether the brain generates consciousness or acts as a transceiver for it. The discussion touches on psilocybin's effects on the default mode network and near-death experiences as research areas for understanding consciousness.

key_claims.claim: Psilocybin silences activity in the default mode network, specifically the anterior and posterior cingulate of the brain
The default mode network is where the ego would be housed if located in any part of the brain, containing fears about the future and worries about the past
Subduing the default mode network may increase the ability to receive wisdom or information from elsewhere
Near-death states, whether at end of life, through trauma, or purposefully induced, are valuable border areas for consciousness research
